title: Pedilonum keytsianum
intro: Pedilonum keytsianum, also known as Peddiea keytsiana or Pedilium keytsianum, is a plant species in the family Clusiaceae, commonly referred to as a flowering shrub or small tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as being used to treat various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Pedilonum keytsianum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Pedilonum keytsianum include decoctions and infusions, as well as extracts in the form of capsules or tinctures, which are used to harness the plant's medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Pedilonum keytsianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Pedilonum and is native to New Guinea. The plant is classified as a member of the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. Within the Rubiaceae family, Pedilonum keytsianum is classified in the order Lamiales. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and order.
